articles.full_text:
60 kmph winds, heavy rain lash Lucknow:Power cuts hit city, heatwave conditions ease across UP; mercury drops 11°C in 5 days
- Copy link
Weather took a dramatic turn in Lucknow on Thursday night, with strong winds blowing at speeds of up to 60 kmph, followed by heavy rain. The intense storm led to power outages in several areas, while tin roofs and temporary structures were blown away.
After 9 pm, electricity was disrupted in many localities including Alambagh, Krishnanagar, Mohanlalganj, Khadra, BKT and Faizullaganj. Areas such as Hazratganj, Balu Adda, Park Road and Gautam Palli were also affected by power cuts.
The electricity department temporarily suspended supply in Mal, Rahimabad, Kakori, Gopra Mau, Rehman Khera and Nadarganj, before restoring it after around 20 minutes. Breakdowns were also reported on Baba Ka Purwa, Ahibaran Pur and Nishatganj New Hyderabad feeders, affecting nearly one lakh residents.
Strong winds snapped tree branches across the city. The sudden weather change caused trouble for commuters heading home and pedestrians caught outdoors. In Sarojininagar, motorists were seen taking shelter under flyovers to escape the rain. The Meteorological Department had already issued an orange alert for rain and thunderstorms.
On 26 April, the city had recorded a maximum temperature of 43°C. By 5 pm on Thursday, it had dropped to 31.7°C — a sharp fall of around 11.3°C in the last five days.
See 5 pictures-
Maximum temperature remained 31.7 degrees
By 5 pm, the maximum temperature in Lucknow stood at 31.7°C, which was 7.8°C below normal. The minimum temperature was recorded at 23°C, 0.1°C below normal. Maximum humidity reached 75%, while minimum humidity stood at 46%.
On Wednesday, the maximum temperature was 39.3°C, while the minimum was 27.3°C. Maximum humidity was 72%, with minimum humidity at 23%.
Heat wave ends in Lucknow and the state
The Meteorological Department reported that the weather mood has changed in Uttar Pradesh including Lucknow. With a sharp drop in temperature, the state has got relief from severe heat and hot winds. Currently, the hot wind situation in the state has almost ended. Gusty winds at speeds of 50 to 60 km per hour were recorded in Lucknow and many other parts of the state.
Meteorological scientist Atul Kumar Singh said that this spell of unseasonal rain may continue till Friday. Due to this, the temperature in central and eastern Uttar Pradesh areas may drop by 2 to 4 degrees in the next 24 hours.
See 2 morning pictures...
Heatwave ends across Uttar Pradesh
The Meteorological Department said weather conditions have changed across Uttar Pradesh, including Lucknow. A sharp fall in temperatures has brought relief from severe heat and heatwave conditions, which have now almost ended across the state. Gusty winds of 50–60 kmph were recorded in several parts of the state.
Meteorologist Atul Kumar Singh said this spell of unseasonal rain may continue till Friday. As a result, temperatures in central and eastern parts of Uttar Pradesh could drop by another 2 to 4 degrees over the next 24 hours.
Another dip likely after 2–3 days
Rain activity is expected to reduce during the first two to three days of May. After that, a fresh western disturbance is likely to become active on 4–5 May, which may bring another spell of rain across Lucknow and other parts of the state, leading to a further drop in temperatures.
